question: How did the Federal Reserve's monetary policy response to the 2008 financial crisis differ from its response to COVID-19?

sources.title: Measuring the Effectiveness of US Monetary Policy during the COVID-19 Recession
The Federal Reserve's Response to the Global Financial Crisis and Its Long-Term Impact: An Interrupted Time-Series Natural Experimental Analysis
An Efficient Mixture of Deep and Machine Learning Models for COVID-19 and Tuberculosis Detection Using X-Ray Images in Resource Limited Settings
Federal Reserve Communication and the COVID-19 Pandemic

answer: The Federal Reserve's monetary policy response to the 2008 financial crisis differed from its response to the COVID-19 pandemic in several ways. According to [arXiv: 2007.15419v1], the Federal Reserve implemented expansionary monetary policies, including quantitative easing, to boost real activity and calm financial markets during the COVID-19 pandemic. In contrast, during the 2008 financial crisis, the Federal Reserve also implemented quantitative easing, but with a greater focus on stabilizing the financial system [arXiv: 2305.12318v1]. The federal funds rate was lowered to near zero during both periods, but the exact timing and magnitude of the rate changes differed. Unfortunately, I was unable to find more detailed information on the federal funds rate during these periods due to limitations in my search results. [FRED: FEDFUNDS] provides current and recent data on the federal funds effective rate, but does not provide a direct comparison of the rate during the 2008 financial crisis and the COVID-19 pandemic.

### Measuring the Effectiveness of US Monetary Policy during the COVID-19 Recession
**Authors**: Martin Feldkircher, Florian Huber, Michael Pfarrhofer  
**Published**: 2020-07-30  
**URL**: http://arxiv.org/abs/2007.15419v1

The COVID-19 recession that started in March 2020 led to an unprecedented decline in economic activity across the globe. To fight this recession, policy makers in central banks engaged in expansionary monetary policy. This paper asks whether the measures adopted by the US Federal Reserve (Fed) have been effective in boosting real activity and calming financial markets. To ...
